steps.title: Spot the defining phrase
steps.content: The passage contrasts traditional forms ("recognizable as such even as they evolve") with ______ forms that "borrow elements from both fiction and essays" and make "something unconventional." The blank describes forms that resist clear categorization — they lack a fixed, definable shape.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The word needs to mean lacking clear, defined shape or category. Substitute each choice:
  • "amorphous": means without a fixed form or clear structure — perfectly describes forms that blend genres and resist categorization. ✓
  • "neutral": means impartial or taking no side — genre-blending forms are not described as neutral or uncommitted; the word doesn’t capture their unconventional nature. ✗
  • "customary": means usual or traditional — the opposite of what the passage describes; these forms are unconventional by definition. ✗
  • "dispersed": means scattered or spread out — refers to physical distribution, not to a form’s defining characteristics. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
